message:
refactor(core): collapse dangling cx blocks + strip residual asupersync-runtime cfg gates (ft-48xy0)

After ft-xbnl0.2.5 stripped dual-runtime feature gates, many wrapper
functions retained unnecessary inner blocks and redundant `return`
statements. This commit:

- Collapses 166 dangling `{ let cx = ...; return self.foo_with_cx(...); }`
  blocks into flat `let cx = ...; self.foo_with_cx(...)` across 20 files
- Strips 37+3 redundant `return` keywords from cx-binding wrappers
- Removes residual `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` gates from
  ipc.rs, wezterm.rs, cass.rs, vendored.rs, web/server.rs, lib.rs,
  native_events.rs, robot_sdk_contracts.rs, vendored/mux_pool.rs
- Adds back unconditional `use crate::runtime_compat::{RwLock, mpsc,
  task::JoinHandle, watch}` to runtime.rs (removed by batch strip)
- Fixes ipc.rs orphaned `connection_tasks` block from removed Tokio path
- Fixes wezterm.rs `get_text_with_cx` trait disambiguation

Net: -504 lines, cargo check clean (--no-default-features).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
